texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1 Introduction
texts.text: 1 Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Release Notes describe new features and changes to the Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) software that are a part of CPRS GUI version 32c (CPRS v32c), which includes both a GUI executable and a KIDS multi-package build, CPRS V32C COMBINED BUILD, 1.0.
texts.text: Release Notes describe new features and changes to the Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) software that are a part of CPRS GUI version 32c (CPRS v32c), which includes both a GUI executable and a KIDS multi-package build, CPRS V32C COMBINED BUILD, 1.0.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS GUI v32c includes new functionality, namely, the Write Access feature, and defect corrections.
texts.text: CPRS GUI v32c includes new functionality, namely, the Write Access feature, and defect corrections.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Listed below are all the applications involved in this project, along with their patch numbers:
texts.text: Listed below are all the applications involved in this project, along with their patch numbers:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: APPLICATION/VERSION					PATCH
texts.text: APPLICATION/VERSION					PATCH

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ORDER ENTRY/RESULTS REPORTING v3.0		OR*3.0*588
texts.text: ORDER ENTRY/RESULTS REPORTING v3.0		OR*3.0*588

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CLINICAL REMINDERS v2.0				PXRM*2.0*82
texts.text: CLINICAL REMINDERS v2.0				PXRM*2.0*82

texts.label: text
texts.orig: TEXT INTEGRATION UTILITIES v1.0			TIU*1.0*353
texts.text: TEXT INTEGRATION UTILITIES v1.0			TIU*1.0*353

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patches OR*3.0*588, PXRM*2.0*82, and TIU*1.0*353 are being released in the KIDS multi-package build, CPRS V32C COMBINED BUILD 1.0.
texts.text: Patches OR*3.0*588, PXRM*2.0*82, and TIU*1.0*353 are being released in the KIDS multi-package build, CPRS V32C COMBINED BUILD 1.0.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4 This Release
texts.text: 4 This Release

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following sections provide a summary of the new features and functions, enhancements and modifications to the existing software, and any known issues for CPRS GUI v32c.
texts.text: The following sections provide a summary of the new features and functions, enhancements and modifications to the existing software, and any known issues for CPRS GUI v32c.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.1 New Features and Functions Added
texts.text: 4.1 New Features and Functions Added

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This section describes the new functionality added to the CPRS GUI v32c release.
texts.text: This section describes the new functionality added to the CPRS GUI v32c release.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.1.1 CPRS Write Access Functionality
texts.text: 4.1.1 CPRS Write Access Functionality

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS Write Access functionality enables an authorized VistA user to permit or restrict write access for each CPRS tab (Problems, Meds, Orders, Notes, Consults, Surgery and Discharge Summary), and for specific functionality within each tab (for example, Cover Sheet Vitals, Encounters and specific Ordering Display Groups).
texts.text: CPRS Write Access functionality enables an authorized VistA user to permit or restrict write access for each CPRS tab (Problems, Meds, Orders, Notes, Consults, Surgery and Discharge Summary), and for specific functionality within each tab (for example, Cover Sheet Vitals, Encounters and specific Ordering Display Groups).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS Write Access functionality eases the transition from CPRS/VistA to the new Electronic Health Record (EHR) system.  It ensures continuity of patient care and optimizes validation of data displayed in the new EHR.  Write Access functionality exists because Sites transitioning to the new EHR must limit unauthorized write-access in CPRS and VistA.  However, sites that have not yet moved to the new EHR can still restrict Write Access to specific areas of CPRS at the system level, or for individual users or divisions.
texts.text: CPRS Write Access functionality eases the transition from CPRS/VistA to the new Electronic Health Record (EHR) system.  It ensures continuity of patient care and optimizes validation of data displayed in the new EHR.  Write Access functionality exists because Sites transitioning to the new EHR must limit unauthorized write-access in CPRS and VistA.  However, sites that have not yet moved to the new EHR can still restrict Write Access to specific areas of CPRS at the system level, or for individual users or divisions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Details of this enhancement:
texts.text: Details of this enhancement:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Write Access restrictions only impact CPRS.  Downstream applications that interface with CPRS/VistA are not impacted.
texts.text: Write Access restrictions only impact CPRS.  Downstream applications that interface with CPRS/VistA are not impacted.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: New VistA Parameters have been created to enable/restrict write access to each CPRS tab:
texts.text: New VistA Parameters have been created to enable/restrict write access to each CPRS tab:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The new parameters can be set at the Package, System, Division and User Levels.
texts.text: The new parameters can be set at the Package, System, Division and User Levels.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: They allow an authorized user to set the entire chart to “read only”, which enables read capability but restricts write access.
texts.text: They allow an authorized user to set the entire chart to “read only”, which enables read capability but restricts write access.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A user may restrict/enable specific tab functionality such as orders, based on Display Group Name (e.g., Inpatient Medications, Outpatient Pharmacy, Blood Bank).
texts.text: A user may restrict/enable specific tab functionality such as orders, based on Display Group Name (e.g., Inpatient Medications, Outpatient Pharmacy, Blood Bank).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If a restricted orderable is selected, an error message will display, based on the CPRS Write Access Error Message definition.
texts.text: If a restricted orderable is selected, an error message will display, based on the CPRS Write Access Error Message definition.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A new menu option, CPRS Write Access Menu, has been added to the CPRS Configuration (Clin Coord) Menu Option. These menu options manage the new parameters:
texts.text: A new menu option, CPRS Write Access Menu, has been added to the CPRS Configuration (Clin Coord) Menu Option. These menu options manage the new parameters:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: LV CPRS Write Access Display
texts.text: LV CPRS Write Access Display

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: EP CPRS Write Access Editor
texts.text: EP CPRS Write Access Editor

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: CP CPRS Write Access Copy User Settings
texts.text: CP CPRS Write Access Copy User Settings

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: RS CPRS Write Access Remove Settings
texts.text: RS CPRS Write Access Remove Settings

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: VU CPRS Write Access View Single User Settings
texts.text: VU CPRS Write Access View Single User Settings

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Access to View Alerts will be available in VistA.  If a site has transitioned to the new EHR:
texts.text: Access to View Alerts will be available in VistA.  If a site has transitioned to the new EHR:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A user will be able to process all alerts in VistA except the ones for TIU and Orders.
texts.text: A user will be able to process all alerts in VistA except the ones for TIU and Orders.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A user will be able to process all alerts on the CPRS GUI if they have the appropriate Write Access Permissions.  For example, a user who does not have Write Access permission for Orders will not be able to process Orders alerts.
texts.text: A user will be able to process all alerts on the CPRS GUI if they have the appropriate Write Access Permissions.  For example, a user who does not have Write Access permission for Orders will not be able to process Orders alerts.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If the Write Access settings have changed in VistA, the CPRS GUI must be restarted before those updates will take effect.
texts.text: If the Write Access settings have changed in VistA, the CPRS GUI must be restarted before those updates will take effect.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A new CPRS GUI screen, Write Access Permission, lists the tabs and additional functionality where write access is allowed. A user can view the Write Access Permission screen by clicking on the Help menu or New EHR Banner.
texts.text: A new CPRS GUI screen, Write Access Permission, lists the tabs and additional functionality where write access is allowed. A user can view the Write Access Permission screen by clicking on the Help menu or New EHR Banner.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If an organization has migrated to a new EHR, the COVID-19 Identifier banner will be repurposed as a New EHR banner, and will look like the example below:
texts.text: If an organization has migrated to a new EHR, the COVID-19 Identifier banner will be repurposed as a New EHR banner, and will look like the example below:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The CPRS VistA List Manager will be disabled after a site migrates to a new eR EHR.
texts.text: The CPRS VistA List Manager will be disabled after a site migrates to a new eR EHR.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A new option, EHR Orders Transition Report, will list orders that can be acted on.
texts.text: A new option, EHR Orders Transition Report, will list orders that can be acted on.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The PDMP Button on the CPRS “ribbon” bar can no longer be disabled.
texts.text: The PDMP Button on the CPRS “ribbon” bar can no longer be disabled.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.1.2 WebView2Loader.dll
texts.text: 4.1.2 WebView2Loader.dll

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS GUI v32c now includes a new file, WebView2Loader.dll v1.0.1462.37. Because Microsoft Edge has replaced Internet Explorer as the default web browser, updates have been made to provide a consistent user experience when opening and using the Consult Toolbox (CTB) application from within CPRS GUI. The loader DLL assists applications with invoking the WebView2 runtime environment. This file, which will be reviewed and updated to newer versions as required, will be part of the CPRS GUI distribution going forward.
texts.text: CPRS GUI v32c now includes a new file, WebView2Loader.dll v1.0.1462.37. Because Microsoft Edge has replaced Internet Explorer as the default web browser, updates have been made to provide a consistent user experience when opening and using the Consult Toolbox (CTB) application from within CPRS GUI. The loader DLL assists applications with invoking the WebView2 runtime environment. This file, which will be reviewed and updated to newer versions as required, will be part of the CPRS GUI distribution going forward.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC000001236287 - Inpatient Medications - Complex Order Issue
texts.text: INC000001236287 - Inpatient Medications - Complex Order Issue

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When a user tries to discontinue part of a complex order, and one part of the order (child or parent) is locked in Pharmacy, CPRS will allow the user to Discontinue the parts of the order that are NOT locked, leaving the one that was still valid.
texts.text: When a user tries to discontinue part of a complex order, and one part of the order (child or parent) is locked in Pharmacy, CPRS will allow the user to Discontinue the parts of the order that are NOT locked, leaving the one that was still valid.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Added code that will prevent the cancelation of associated orders if one of
texts.text: Added code that will prevent the cancelation of associated orders if one of

texts.label: text
texts.orig: the other associated orders is currently locked.
texts.text: the other associated orders is currently locked.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC R10433527FY16 - PATIENT CARE ENCOUNTERS, historical visit prompts for primary provider erroneously.
texts.text: INC R10433527FY16 - PATIENT CARE ENCOUNTERS, historical visit prompts for primary provider erroneously.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Notes entered for an encounter, where the notes included a reminder dialog, were prompting for a primacy provider even when the note template had been set to suppress that.  Notes that don’t include a reminder don’t have this issue.  This was found to be true whether the note was for a historical encounter or a current one.
texts.text: Notes entered for an encounter, where the notes included a reminder dialog, were prompting for a primacy provider even when the note template had been set to suppress that.  Notes that don’t include a reminder don’t have this issue.  This was found to be true whether the note was for a historical encounter or a current one.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After the user clicks on the “Finish” button for the reminder dialog, but before they are asked if they are the primary provider, CPRS checks if SUPPRESS DX/CPT ON ENTRY is set (Yes) for that note.
texts.text: After the user clicks on the “Finish” button for the reminder dialog, but before they are asked if they are the primary provider, CPRS checks if SUPPRESS DX/CPT ON ENTRY is set (Yes) for that note.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: I15588508FY17 - Error entering delayed orders while patient is admitted
texts.text: I15588508FY17 - Error entering delayed orders while patient is admitted

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the patient is admitted while entering delayed orders for that patient in CPRS, errors will occur that shut CPRS down. The user receives a warning message that the patient has been admitted, then is presented with a question if they want to continue adding new orders.  Both choices lead to access violation errors that will cause CPRS to close.
texts.text: If the patient is admitted while entering delayed orders for that patient in CPRS, errors will occur that shut CPRS down. The user receives a warning message that the patient has been admitted, then is presented with a question if they want to continue adding new orders.  Both choices lead to access violation errors that will cause CPRS to close.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When CPRS detects the completed admission while entering orders, it resets
texts.text: When CPRS detects the completed admission while entering orders, it resets

texts.label: text
texts.orig: the delayed event type to the proper code ('C', for current/no longer delayed) instead of a null character (#0, which will crash the RPC broker).
texts.text: the delayed event type to the proper code ('C', for current/no longer delayed) instead of a null character (#0, which will crash the RPC broker).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: R18218636FY18 - User states that no one in the Pharmacy can access this one patient file in CPRS, shows locked
texts.text: R18218636FY18 - User states that no one in the Pharmacy can access this one patient file in CPRS, shows locked

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When a patient has a DFN with a decimal point (".") in it, their demographic information cannot be viewed in CPRS.
texts.text: When a patient has a DFN with a decimal point (".") in it, their demographic information cannot be viewed in CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In CPRS, before the demographics form was displayed, the patient's DFN was converted from a string to a 64-bit integer. Since the DFN had a decimal point, it was not a valid integer, which caused the form not to be displayed. The code was changed to convert the DFN string a floating-point number. The point of the conversion is to verify that the DFN wasn't blank, zero, or negative.
texts.text: In CPRS, before the demographics form was displayed, the patient's DFN was converted from a string to a 64-bit integer. Since the DFN had a decimal point, it was not a valid integer, which caused the form not to be displayed. The code was changed to convert the DFN string a floating-point number. The point of the conversion is to verify that the DFN wasn't blank, zero, or negative.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: I9836510FY16 - BL: Text Integration Utility - Other
texts.text: I9836510FY16 - BL: Text Integration Utility - Other

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When a provider creates a discharge summary for which he is also the attending physician, when he attempts to sign the discharge, he is shown the warning: "Author has not signed, are you SURE you want to sign?"
texts.text: When a provider creates a discharge summary for which he is also the attending physician, when he attempts to sign the discharge, he is shown the warning: "Author has not signed, are you SURE you want to sign?"

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the author (dictator) and attending are the same as the provider attempting to sign the discharge summary, do not check to see if the author has signed yet (he hasn't) and do not show this warning.
texts.text: If the author (dictator) and attending are the same as the provider attempting to sign the discharge summary, do not check to see if the author has signed yet (he hasn't) and do not show this warning.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: I7509280FY16 - SAM: CPRS - Lab Collect Order entered after cut off time
texts.text: I7509280FY16 - SAM: CPRS - Lab Collect Order entered after cut off time

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The provider didn't select a collection date/time from the drop-down list. Instead, they entered a collection date/time that was technically valid and in the future. However, the cutoff time for that collection had already passed and the provider was given no warning.
texts.text: The provider didn't select a collection date/time from the drop-down list. Instead, they entered a collection date/time that was technically valid and in the future. However, the cutoff time for that collection had already passed and the provider was given no warning.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If a date/time for collection is entered for which the cutoff time has already passed, CPRS will not allow the entry and provide this message to the user: "The cutoff time has passed for the selected lab collection time. Choose another collection time."
texts.text: If a date/time for collection is entered for which the cutoff time has already passed, CPRS will not allow the entry and provide this message to the user: "The cutoff time has passed for the selected lab collection time. Choose another collection time."

texts.label: text
texts.orig: I9926255FY16 - Meds not viewable in orders tab
texts.text: I9926255FY16 - Meds not viewable in orders tab

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When transferring an outpatient medication to inpatient with a manual release action, the order no longer shows up on the Orders tab.
texts.text: When transferring an outpatient medication to inpatient with a manual release action, the order no longer shows up on the Orders tab.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The CPRS GUI was updated to correctly transfer these orders over when performing a manual release action.
texts.text: The CPRS GUI was updated to correctly transfer these orders over when performing a manual release action.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC19398646 9/3 notes JAWS issue
texts.text: INC19398646 9/3 notes JAWS issue

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the meds tab of CPRS, when searching for a new medication, JAWS is not able to read anything from the list. it's completely visually inaccessible.
texts.text: In the meds tab of CPRS, when searching for a new medication, JAWS is not able to read anything from the list. it's completely visually inaccessible.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The CPRS GUI was updated to correct JAWS's ability to read the list.
texts.text: The CPRS GUI was updated to correct JAWS's ability to read the list.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC21075957 CPRS hover feature is not accessible
texts.text: INC21075957 CPRS hover feature is not accessible

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS is using a hover feature to show whether patients are registered in My HealtheVet to send Secure Messages. If you hover over the My HealtheVet button, it will turn blue if they are registered. For JAWS users, the button just isn't available, but I have to tab over 10 times to determine whether the button is there or not. There is simply no way to know this unless one knows the exact location of this button in the tab order. Original problem was entered in as a request #REQ6354081.
texts.text: CPRS is using a hover feature to show whether patients are registered in My HealtheVet to send Secure Messages. If you hover over the My HealtheVet button, it will turn blue if they are registered. For JAWS users, the button just isn't available, but I have to tab over 10 times to determine whether the button is there or not. There is simply no way to know this unless one knows the exact location of this button in the tab order. Original problem was entered in as a request #REQ6354081.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The CPRS GUI was updated to correct handling of the hover feature.
texts.text: The CPRS GUI was updated to correct handling of the hover feature.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC24901775 CPRS V32B :User is not able to print on CPRS
texts.text: INC24901775 CPRS V32B :User is not able to print on CPRS

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When Printing a consult SF513 to a network printer an erroneous error message "0^Queued as task #" is displayed. While this error did not actually affect/stop printing, its appearance created confusion for users.
texts.text: When Printing a consult SF513 to a network printer an erroneous error message "0^Queued as task #" is displayed. While this error did not actually affect/stop printing, its appearance created confusion for users.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This erroneous error message has been removed.
texts.text: This erroneous error message has been removed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC26701552 Error message when starting new note (duplicates: INC26651638, INC26613580 and INC26629746)
texts.text: INC26701552 Error message when starting new note (duplicates: INC26651638, INC26613580 and INC26629746)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After OR*3.0*598 was released, creating a new note in CPRS with an attached Reminder Dialog template would cause an access violation, preventing the opening of the template.
texts.text: After OR*3.0*598 was released, creating a new note in CPRS with an attached Reminder Dialog template would cause an access violation, preventing the opening of the template.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The CPRS GUI was updated to correctly create the dialog without an access violation.
texts.text: The CPRS GUI was updated to correctly create the dialog without an access violation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC26523979 - RELATED TO THE REPORTED CPRS Pt Record Flag (PRF) Error - Pt Safety Alert N23-01 ISSUE, FURTHER OCCURENCES - HITPS-10039.
texts.text: INC26523979 - RELATED TO THE REPORTED CPRS Pt Record Flag (PRF) Error - Pt Safety Alert N23-01 ISSUE, FURTHER OCCURENCES - HITPS-10039.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A CPRS GUI user selected a flagged patient and the correct patient record flag displayed.  That same user highlighted another flagged patient from the Patient Selection screen but clicked the Cancel button. The patient record for the first flagged patient still displayed.  However, the patient record flag displayed the flag data of the highlighted but unselected patient.
texts.text: A CPRS GUI user selected a flagged patient and the correct patient record flag displayed.  That same user highlighted another flagged patient from the Patient Selection screen but clicked the Cancel button. The patient record for the first flagged patient still displayed.  However, the patient record flag displayed the flag data of the highlighted but unselected patient.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The patient record flag displays the correct flag data.
texts.text: The patient record flag displays the correct flag data.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC25144966 – Email links not opening Outlook for some users
texts.text: INC25144966 – Email links not opening Outlook for some users

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS GUI templates may contain mailto links. It was reported in CPRS v32b that, for some users, mailto links no longer open in Outlook. For some users, the mailto link will first open Edge browser, and then open a new Outlook message. For other users, the mailto link only opens Edge, a new Outlook message is never opened.
texts.text: CPRS GUI templates may contain mailto links. It was reported in CPRS v32b that, for some users, mailto links no longer open in Outlook. For some users, the mailto link will first open Edge browser, and then open a new Outlook message. For other users, the mailto link only opens Edge, a new Outlook message is never opened.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The tool being used to honor mailto links was modified. According to Microsoft, the function previously used was altered/unavailable in subsequent versions of the operating system or product.
texts.text: The tool being used to honor mailto links was modified. According to Microsoft, the function previously used was altered/unavailable in subsequent versions of the operating system or product.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: INC25242144 – Template Editor up and down arrows can be hidden
texts.text: INC25242144 – Template Editor up and down arrows can be hidden

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Problem:
texts.text: Problem:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After the installation of CPRS v32b, the up and down arrow buttons in the  template editor are grayed out when the font is set to 10.
texts.text: After the installation of CPRS v32b, the up and down arrow buttons in the  template editor are grayed out when the font is set to 10.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The template editor was modified in the CPRS GUI to properly display the arrows when fonts are adjusted.
texts.text: The template editor was modified in the CPRS GUI to properly display the arrows when fonts are adjusted.

texts.label: text

texts.text: Unable to Park supply orders on the Orders tab.

texts.text: Problem:

texts.orig: Users were unable to park supply orders when working on the Orders tab, even though Park was enabled.
texts.text: Users were unable to park supply orders when working on the Orders tab, even though Park was enabled.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution:
texts.text: Resolution:

texts.label: text

texts.text: Updated functionality on the Orders tab to allow supply orders to be parked.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5 New Parameters
texts.text: 5 New Parameters

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS v32c has several new parameters.
texts.text: CPRS v32c has several new parameters.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5.1 CPRS Parameters
texts.text: 5.1 CPRS Parameters

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OR CPRS ORDERS WRITE ACCESS:
texts.text: OR CPRS ORDERS WRITE ACCESS:

texts.label: text
texts.orig:
This parameter allows a site to control whether or not a user, a division, or the entire system (site) is allowed to write orders by assigning write privilege by order display groups. This parameter is only evaluated if the user has access to the Orders tab based on the parameter settings in the parameter OR CPRS TABS WRITE ACCESS. The parameter also has a package level but it should not be set by the site.

If the display group parameter is defined at a higher precedence level, the value will be passed down to the lower level if a lower level does not overwrite it.
texts.text:
This parameter allows a site to control whether or not a user, a division, or the entire system (site) is allowed to write orders by assigning write privilege by order display groups. This parameter is only evaluated if the user has access to the Orders tab based on the parameter settings in the parameter OR CPRS TABS WRITE ACCESS. The parameter also has a package level but it should not be set by the site.

If the display group parameter is defined at a higher precedence level, the value will be passed down to the lower level if a lower level does not overwrite it.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OR CPRS OTHER WRITE ACCESS –
texts.text: OR CPRS OTHER WRITE ACCESS –

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This parameter allows the site to control the CPRS write access for some miscellaneous functions. The control can be at the user, division, or system (site). The functions that can be defined are: A for Allergies, D for Delayed Orders, E for Encounters, I for Immunizations, R for the Reminder Editor, and W for Women's Health. The parameter also has a package level but this should not be set by the site.
texts.text: This parameter allows the site to control the CPRS write access for some miscellaneous functions. The control can be at the user, division, or system (site). The functions that can be defined are: A for Allergies, D for Delayed Orders, E for Encounters, I for Immunizations, R for the Reminder Editor, and W for Women's Health. The parameter also has a package level but this should not be set by the site.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OR CPRS TABS WRITE ACCESS –
texts.text: OR CPRS TABS WRITE ACCESS –

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This parameter allows the site to control the CPRS  'tabs' write access. The control can be at the user, division, system (site), or package level. The tabs that can be defined are: C for Consults, D for Discharge Summary, M for Meds, O for orders, P for Problems, or S for Surgery.
texts.text: This parameter allows the site to control the CPRS  'tabs' write access. The control can be at the user, division, system (site), or package level. The tabs that can be defined are: C for Consults, D for Discharge Summary, M for Meds, O for orders, P for Problems, or S for Surgery.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OR CPRS WRITE ACCESS ERROR:
texts.text: OR CPRS WRITE ACCESS ERROR:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This error message tells the CPRS user why they are not allowed to perform an attempted action. For example: if write access is restricted and a user attempts to create an order.
texts.text: This error message tells the CPRS user why they are not allowed to perform an attempted action. For example: if write access is restricted and a user attempts to create an order.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OR MHV URL:
texts.text: OR MHV URL:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This parameter contains the website URL that VistA will access after the user clicks on the MHV Button in the CPRS GUI.
texts.text: This parameter contains the website URL that VistA will access after the user clicks on the MHV Button in the CPRS GUI.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OR SIMULATE ON EHR:
texts.text: OR SIMULATE ON EHR:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This parameter can be used to simulate as if the site migrated to an Electronic Health Record (EHR) system. This will only work in the test/pre-prod account. When set to YES, it will cause the ONEHR^ORACCESS API to return a "1", as if the site is on EHR. It should only be used for testing purposes.
texts.text: This parameter can be used to simulate as if the site migrated to an Electronic Health Record (EHR) system. This will only work in the test/pre-prod account. When set to YES, it will cause the ONEHR^ORACCESS API to return a "1", as if the site is on EHR. It should only be used for testing purposes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ORWCH PAUSE INPUT:
texts.text: ORWCH PAUSE INPUT:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The purpose of this parameter is to allow control whether or not the Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) will pause the processing of user input while Windows messages are being processed. When this doesn’t occur, access violation errors may happen.
texts.text: The purpose of this parameter is to allow control whether or not the Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) will pause the processing of user input while Windows messages are being processed. When this doesn’t occur, access violation errors may happen.
